topic: cricket -- India vs England test match opinions

turns.content: mate did you see the india england test yesterday absolute scenes
I didn't catch it live, but I've seen some of the highlights. What happened that's got you this fired up?

bumrah was unplayable. genuinely unplayable. five wickets in the first innings and england just had no answer. root tried to hold it together but the rest of them were clueless. bazball doesnt work when youve got bumrah running in at you does it

Bumrah at his best is one of the hardest bowlers to face in world cricket right now. That unusual action makes the ball arrive from an angle batsmen just can't prepare for. Five wickets is a statement. Did Root put up much of a fight or did he go cheaply too?

root got 40 odd which is decent but no one stayed with him. thats the problem with england innit they rely on root and when he goes the whole thing falls apart. india just have so much more depth. kohli pant gill rahul. even the lower order can bat

That depth is what separates the top sides. India can lose two or three key wickets and still post a competitive total because there's quality all the way down. England's over-reliance on Root has been a problem for years -- they need someone else to consistently step up. How did Kohli go in this one?

kohli got a fifty. proper kohli knock too, watchful at the start then opened up. him and pant put on about 120 for the fourth wicket and that was basically the match done. pant was mental as usual smacking it everywhere but it works for him doesnt it

Pant's style shouldn't work in Test cricket but somehow it does. That fearless approach puts the bowling side under pressure because they never know what's coming. A Kohli fifty and a Pant counterattack is pretty much the nightmare scenario for any opposition. Do you think India can keep this form going for the rest of the series?

yeah I reckon 3-1 to india at least. england might nick one test if the conditions suit them but theyre not winning the series. and warwickshire have got two of the india lads training at edgbaston this summer which is brilliant for the county. might try and get down to watch them

That would be a great opportunity to see some quality cricket up close at Edgbaston. County sides hosting international players always get a boost from it, and Warwickshire could use the quality if they're in the mix for the Championship. Do you get to many matches at Edgbaston during the summer?

try to get to a couple when I can yeah. t20 nights are good cos I can go after work. the ipl is on right now too so im staying up late watching that. mumbai indians are having a shocker this year though its painful. I support them cos of rohit but theyre bottom of the table

Mumbai Indians have been inconsistent the last couple of seasons -- the core group that won all those titles has aged and the replacements haven't quite filled the gap yet. T20 nights at Edgbaston sound like a good shout though, especially after a day's work. Who do you think takes the IPL this year if not Mumbai?

KKR or CSK probably. KKR look solid this year and CSK just always find a way innit. dhoni might be done but they still have that winning mentality. do you reckon IPL has made test cricket worse though? like everyone just wants to smack it now

It's definitely changed the way batsmen think. The fearless approach filters through -- players who grow up playing franchise T20 cricket are more willing to attack from ball one, even in Tests. Whether that's worse or just different is debatable. Purists hate it, but a Pant-style innings in a Test match is genuinely exciting to watch. The concern is more about scheduling -- players choosing IPL contracts over Test tours. That's where the real tension is.

yeah thats fair. I still love test cricket the most though. theres nothing like a five day game going down to the wire. anyway mate cheers for the cricket chat needed that. back to fixing a combi boiler now haha

Test cricket at its best is unbeatable -- nothing else in sport has that kind of slow-burn drama. Good luck with the combi boiler, and enjoy the IPL tonight. Hopefully Mumbai Indians can start turning things around.
